coaching.title: Today's Run
coaching.message: Long run day in peak phase—this is your biggest stimulus before taper. Target 90–100 min on trails with 800–1,200m of vert; aim to hit some sustained climbing at Z3 (aerobic threshold, ~160–170 bpm) in the middle 40 min, then recover pace for the final descent. Julian Alps demands relentless leg strength on 35–50% grades, so don't drift into comfort; you need to practice holding power when tired. If you're traveling this week for work, adapt: find a local climb that mimics the terrain (sustained, steep, technical foot placement) rather than a flat out-and-back.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: You're 90+ min on vert—consume 60–80g carbs during the run (gels, sports drink, real food if you tolerate it). Start fueling at 30 min in, not at the wall. Post-run (within 30 min), hit 30–40g protein + 80–120g carbs to spike insulin and drive glycogen repletion; your legs will thank you on recovery rides mid-week. Given your travel schedule, pre-stage your fuel the night before so you're not scrambling in a hotel or airport.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: You're deep in peak phase on a non-recovery week—prioritize 7.5–8.5 hrs sleep tonight and keep Sunday intentionally easy (Z1 shuffle, 40–50 min max). No second hard effort this weekend. Use a foam roller on calves and quads for 8–10 min post-run (not massage gun yet—too aggressive). If you're working on a deal at MNTN this week, mentally ring-fence the evening post-run for sleep prep, not emails.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You have 104 days to race—taper is in 3 weeks. This long run is your chance to affirm you *can* sustain vert work and hold power when fatigued, which is exactly what will break others in Slovenia. Run with intention, not just volume. Note how your body responds on the final 15 min of climbing: if legs feel strong and mind is calm, you're primed. If you're struggling mechanically or mentally, that's data to bring to your Tuesday quality session.
